quotes.text: I never knew what pretense Salem was, I never knew the lying lessons I was taught by all these Christian women and their covenanted men!
quotes.speaker: Abigail Williams
quotes.context: Abigail to Proctor in the Parris bedroom, after he tells her their affair is finished.

quotes.text: I will come to you in the black of some terrible night and I will bring a pointy reckoning that will shudder you.
quotes.speaker: Abigail Williams
quotes.context: Abigail threatening Mary Warren and Betty into silence.

quotes.text: I saw Indians smash my dear parents' heads on the pillow next to mine.
quotes.speaker: Abigail Williams
quotes.context: Abigail to the girls, claiming the moral authority of trauma to enforce silence.

quotes.text: I want to open myself! I want the light of God, I want the sweet love of Jesus!
quotes.speaker: Abigail Williams
quotes.context: Abigail's first false confession, immediately followed by accusations against named townspeople.

quotes.text: I have laid seven babies unbaptized in the earth.
quotes.speaker: Mrs. Ann Putnam
quotes.context: Mrs. Putnam justifying having sent her daughter Ruth to Tituba to conjure the spirits of her dead sisters.

quotes.text: I am one of nine sons; the Putnam seed have peopled this province.
quotes.speaker: Thomas Putnam
quotes.context: Putnam asserting his family's social position over Rebecca Nurse's restraint.

quotes.text: There is a faction that is sworn to drive me from my pulpit.
quotes.speaker: Reverend Parris
quotes.context: Parris to Abigail, exposing his preoccupation with his own standing rather than his daughter.

quotes.text: Abby, that's done with.
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor refusing Abigail's first attempt to revive their affair in the Parris bedroom.

quotes.text: Aye, but we did not.
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor denying the affair to Abigail in a brief exchange where she asserts "Aye, but we did."

quotes.text: I do not judge you. The magistrate sits in your heart that judges you. I never thought you but a good man, John, only somewhat bewildered.
quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Proctor
quotes.context: Elizabeth to John in the common room of the Proctor house, when he accuses her of unending judgement.

quotes.text: Your justice would freeze beer!
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor to Elizabeth, lashing out at the relentlessness of her unspoken judgement.

quotes.text: Adultery, John.
quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Proctor
quotes.context: Elizabeth quietly supplying the commandment Proctor cannot recall while Hale tests them.

quotes.text: Theology, sir, is a fortress; no crack in a fortress may be accounted small.
quotes.speaker: Reverend Hale
quotes.context: Hale interrogating Proctor's lapsed church attendance.

quotes.text: Vengeance is walking Salem... common vengeance writes the law!
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor at his door, refusing to surrender Elizabeth's warrant after Cheever arrives.

quotes.text: We are only what we always were, but naked now.
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor, at the curtain of Act 2, wrenching Mary Warren toward the truth as the reckoning closes in.

quotes.text: A person is either with this court or he must be counted against it, there be no road between.
quotes.speaker: Deputy Governor Danforth
quotes.context: Danforth warning Francis Nurse against his attempt to defend his wife.

quotes.text: Witchcraft is ipso facto, on its face and by its nature, an invisible crime, is it not? Therefore, who may possibly be witness to it? The witch and the victim. None other.
quotes.speaker: Deputy Governor Danforth
quotes.context: Danforth defending the court's reliance on the children as the only possible witnesses.

quotes.text: I have known her, sir. I have known her.
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor confessing the affair with Abigail in open court to discredit her.

quotes.text: It is a whore's vengeance, and you must see it.
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor naming Abigail's motive after his confession.

quotes.text: No, sir.
quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Proctor
quotes.context: Elizabeth's lie to Danforth, denying she knows John as a lecher, in a bid to save his name.

quotes.text: I say, I say, God is dead!
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor's outburst as Mary turns on him and the court closes in.

quotes.text: I denounce these proceedings, I quit this court!
quotes.speaker: Reverend Hale
quotes.context: Hale walking out at the curtain of Act 3, his arc from confident witch-hunter to dissenter complete.

quotes.text: There is blood on my head! Can you not see the blood on my head!
quotes.speaker: Reverend Hale
quotes.context: Hale, returned to Salem, confessing his guilt to Danforth.

quotes.text: Life, woman, life is God's most precious gift; no principle, however glorious, may justify the taking of it.
quotes.speaker: Reverend Hale
quotes.context: Hale begging Elizabeth to persuade Proctor to confess and live.

quotes.text: More weight.
quotes.speaker: Giles Corey (reported by Elizabeth)
quotes.context: Elizabeth recounting how Giles, refusing to plead, was pressed to death and gave the executioners only those two words.

quotes.text: It needs a cold wife to prompt lechery.
quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Proctor
quotes.context: Elizabeth taking her share of responsibility for the failure of their marriage in the jail cell.

quotes.text: Suspicion kissed you when I did; I never knew how I should say my love. It were a cold house I kept!
quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Proctor
quotes.context: Elizabeth's confession to John before he chooses whether to sign.

quotes.text: I cannot mount the gibbet like a saint. It is a fraud. I am not that man.
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor explaining to Elizabeth why he is leaning toward confession.

quotes.text: Because it is my name! Because I cannot have another in my life!
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor refusing to let Danforth post his confession on the church door.

quotes.text: How may I live without my name? I have given you my soul; leave me my name!
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor's final plea before tearing the confession.

quotes.text: I do think I see some shred of goodness in John Proctor. Not enough to weave a banner with, but white enough to keep it from such dogs.
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor after tearing the confession, recovering a moral position he had thought lost.

quotes.text: He have his goodness now. God forbid I take it from him!
quotes.speaker: Elizabeth Proctor
quotes.context: Elizabeth's final words as Proctor is led out, refusing Hale's plea that she beg him to recant.

quotes.text: I'll not be ordered to bed no more, Mr. Proctor! I am eighteen and a woman, however single!
quotes.speaker: Mary Warren
quotes.context: Mary Warren, returned from court with new public authority, asserting herself in the Proctor house.

quotes.text: You're the Devil's man!
quotes.speaker: Mary Warren
quotes.context: Mary Warren breaking under Abigail's yellow-bird performance and turning on Proctor in court.

quotes.text: I will fall like an ocean on that court!
quotes.speaker: John Proctor
quotes.context: Proctor to the arrested Elizabeth, promising to bring down the court that has taken her.
